name: Goblet Squat
setupSteps: Hold one dumbbell vertically at chest height, cupping the top end with both hands.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes turned out slightly.
Keep elbows tucked in front of your chest.
executionSteps: Take a breath and brace your core.
Lower by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, keeping your chest up.
Descend until thighs are parallel to the floor or as deep as comfortable.
Drive through your whole foot to stand back up.
Exhale at the top.
coachingCues: Chest proud — don't let it collapse forward.
Knees track over your toes, not caving inward.
Sit between your hips, not behind them.
commonMistakes: Heels lifting off the floor
Knees collapsing inward
Rounding the lower back at the bottom
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp knee or back pain.
If balance is an issue, stand near a wall or chair for support.

name: Bodyweight Squat
setupSteps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes turned out slightly.
Arms can extend forward for balance, or fold across the chest.
executionSteps: Push your hips back as if sitting into a chair.
Bend your knees and lower until thighs are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep your chest tall and weight in your whole foot.
Drive up to standing.
coachingCues: Imagine sitting back into a chair behind you.
Knees in line with your toes.
Squeeze your glutes at the top.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward
Heels lifting
Going too fast and losing form

name: Box Squat (to chair)
setupSteps: Stand in front of a sturdy chair, bench, or box, feet shoulder-width apart.
Toes pointed slightly out.
executionSteps: Lower yourself slowly to lightly touch the chair with your seat.
Don't fully sit down — pause briefly when you make contact.
Stand back up by driving through your feet.
coachingCues: Control the descent — don't drop onto the chair.
Chest up throughout.
commonMistakes: Crashing onto the chair
Using arms to push off the chair to stand

name: Dumbbell Split Squat
setupSteps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Take a long step forward into a split stance — front foot flat, back heel raised.
Stand tall with hips square.
executionSteps: Lower straight down by bending both knees.
Aim for the back knee to gently touch or hover over the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to standing.
Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
coachingCues: Keep your torso upright — don't lean forward.
Front knee tracks over the front foot, not past it.
Most of the weight stays on the front leg.
commonMistakes: Stance too short, putting strain on the front knee
Pushing off the back foot too much
Rocking forward instead of straight down

name: Split Squat (Bodyweight)
setupSteps: Step into a split stance, one foot forward, one back.
Hands on hips or held in front for balance.
executionSteps: Lower straight down, both knees bending.
Back knee tracks toward the floor.
Drive through the front foot to stand.
All reps on one side, then switch.
coachingCues: Stay upright — don't pitch forward.
Front knee over front foot.
commonMistakes: Stance too short
Pushing off the back leg

name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
setupSteps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
executionSteps: Step one foot back and lower into a lunge — front thigh parallel, back knee toward the floor.
Drive through the front foot to return to the start.
Alternate legs each rep, or complete all on one side first.
coachingCues: Step back far enough to get a clean 90° in both knees.
Front knee stays over the front foot, not past it.
Torso upright throughout.
commonMistakes: Stepping too short and stressing the front knee
Letting the torso fall forward
Rushing the reps and losing balance

name: Reverse Lunge (Bodyweight)
setupSteps: Stand tall, feet hip-width apart.
Hands on hips or held forward for balance.
executionSteps: Step one foot back into a lunge.
Drop the back knee toward the floor.
Push through the front foot to return.
Alternate legs.
coachingCues: Long step back, not short.
Torso vertical.
commonMistakes: Forward knee travelling too far past the toes
Wobbling — slow down if you do

name: Step-Up
setupSteps: Find a sturdy step, bench, or box at roughly knee height.
Stand facing it, feet hip-width apart.
executionSteps: Place one foot fully on the step.
Drive through that foot to stand all the way up.
Step back down the same side, controlled.
All reps on one side, then switch.
coachingCues: Push through the heel of the stepping foot.
Don't push off the floor with the trailing leg — let the working leg do all the work.
Stand fully tall at the top.
commonMistakes: Using momentum from the bottom leg
Step that's too high — start lower

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
setupSteps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body.
Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft (slightly bent, not locked).
executionSteps: Push your hips back as if closing a car door behind you.
Lower the weights down the front of your legs — they should brush your shins or stay close.
Stop when you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings (usually around mid-shin).
Drive your hips forward to return to standing.
Squeeze your glutes at the top.
coachingCues: It's a hip movement, not a knee bend.
Keep the back flat — long line from your head to your tailbone.
Weights stay close to your legs the whole way.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back
Bending the knees too much (turning it into a squat)
Letting the dumbbells drift away from the body
safetyNotes: If you feel this in your lower back, stop and reduce range or weight.
Should be felt in the back of the legs.

name: Hip Hinge (Bodyweight)
setupSteps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent.
Hands across your chest or on your hips.
executionSteps: Push your hips back as far as comfortable.
Let your torso fold forward, keeping the back flat.
Stop when you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to stand tall.
coachingCues: Hips move back — don't just bend forward.
Long spine, no rounding.
commonMistakes: Rounding the back
Bending the knees instead of hinging

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
setupSteps: Stand on one leg, soft bend in the standing knee.
Optional: hold a light dumbbell in the opposite hand for counterbalance.
executionSteps: Hinge at the hips, letting the free leg extend straight back behind you.
Body forms a straight line from head to back heel.
Lower until you feel a stretch in the standing leg's hamstring.
Drive the standing-leg hip forward to return upright.
coachingCues: Imagine your back leg and torso are one straight stick.
Hips stay square — don't twist open.
Slow and controlled — balance is the challenge.
commonMistakes: Hips opening sideways
Bending the standing knee too much
Going too fast and losing balance

name: Glute Bridge
setupSteps: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart.
Heels close enough that fingertips can almost touch them.
Arms relaxed at your sides.
executionSteps: Squeeze your glutes and drive your hips up.
Form a straight line from knees to shoulders at the top.
Pause and squeeze for a beat.
Lower with control.
coachingCues: Push the floor away with your heels.
Feel it in the glutes, not the lower back.
Don't over-arch at the top — straight line, not banana shape.
commonMistakes: Pushing through the toes instead of the heels
Hyperextending and arching the lower back at the top

name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
setupSteps: Sit on the floor with your upper back against the long edge of a bench.
Place a dumbbell across your hip crease (use a folded towel for comfort).
Feet flat, hip-width apart, knees bent at about 90° at the top of the movement.
executionSteps: Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Drive your hips up until your torso is parallel to the floor.
Pause and squeeze for one second.
Lower with control without letting the weight rest fully on the floor between reps.
coachingCues: Tuck your chin slightly — eyes look at the dumbbell, not the ceiling.
Feel the squeeze in your glutes at the top.
Knees stay over your ankles, not collapsing in or flaring out.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back instead of finishing with the glutes
Knees caving inward
Using a bench that's too tall (shoulders too high) — should be just below the shoulder blades
safetyNotes: Place the dumbbell carefully — they can pinch. A pad or folded towel makes a big difference.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
setupSteps: Sit on the bench with a dumbbell on each thigh.
Lie back, using your knees to help kick the weights up to chest level.
Feet flat on the floor, dumbbells held above your chest with palms facing your feet.
executionSteps: Lower the dumbbells under control to chest level — slightly wider than shoulders.
Pause briefly when your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Press the weights up and slightly toward each other until arms are extended (don't lock out hard).
Repeat.
coachingCues: Wrists stacked over elbows.
Lower with control — about 2 seconds down.
Squeeze your chest at the top.
commonMistakes: Bouncing the weights at the bottom
Flaring elbows out to 90° — keep them at about 45° from the body
Arching the lower back excessively
safetyNotes: Use weights you can fully control — no need to fail solo without a spotter.
If bench-pressing without a partner, stay conservative.

name: Dumbbell Floor Press
setupSteps: Lie on the floor with knees bent, feet flat.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest, palms facing your feet.
executionSteps: Lower the weights until your upper arms touch the floor.
Pause briefly.
Press back up to the starting position.
coachingCues: Elbows tucked to about 45°.
Floor sets a natural depth limit — use it.
commonMistakes: Slamming the elbows into the floor
Bouncing off the floor

name: Push-Up
setupSteps: Hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Feet together or hip-width apart.
Body forms a straight line from head to heels.
executionSteps: Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Lower under control until your chest is just above the floor.
Press back up by pushing the floor away.
Maintain the straight body line throughout.
coachingCues: Hands stack under shoulders, not in front.
Elbows at about 45°, not flared straight out.
Squeeze glutes — don't let hips sag.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging
Hips piking up (butt in the air)
Half-reps — chest barely moves

name: Kneeling Push-Up
setupSteps: Kneel on the floor, hands shoulder-width apart in front of you.
Walk hands forward until your body forms a straight line from head to knees.
Knees act as the pivot point instead of toes.
executionSteps: Lower your chest toward the floor with control.
Press back up to the starting position.
Keep the straight line from knees to head throughout.
coachingCues: Same body-line discipline as a full push-up — knees down doesn't mean hips sag.
Elbows about 45°.
commonMistakes: Sitting back onto the heels at the top
Hips sagging or piking

name: Incline Push-Up
setupSteps: Place hands on a sturdy surface — countertop, bench, sturdy chair, or wall.
Step feet back so your body is at an angle, straight line from head to heels.
The higher the surface, the easier the push-up.
executionSteps: Lower chest to the surface under control.
Press back up.
coachingCues: Pick the height that lets you do 8–12 clean reps.
As you get stronger, lower the surface.
commonMistakes: Surface too low too soon
Hips sagging or piking

name: Decline Push-Up
setupSteps: Hands on the floor, feet elevated on a sturdy bench, step, or chair.
Body forms a straight line from head to heels.
executionSteps: Lower under control until your chest is just above the floor.
Press back up.
Maintain the straight line.
coachingCues: The higher your feet, the harder it gets — don't go too high too fast.
Core stays braced throughout.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging
Going too high too fast and losing form

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press (Seated)
setupSteps: Sit on a bench with back support, feet flat.
Hold dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows bent.
Wrists stacked over elbows.
executionSteps: Brace your core.
Press the dumbbells overhead until arms are nearly extended.
Lower with control to the starting position.
coachingCues: Don't over-arch the lower back — ribs down.
Elbows under wrists throughout the press.
Press up and slightly together at the top.
commonMistakes: Excessive lower back arch
Going too heavy and losing the press path
Locking elbows hard at the top
safetyNotes: If you have shoulder issues, try a neutral grip (palms facing each other) instead.

name: Pike Push-Up
setupSteps: Start in a downward-dog shape — hands and feet on the floor, hips high, body forms an inverted V.
Hands shoulder-width, feet hip-width.
executionSteps: Bend the elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Press back up to the starting V shape.
coachingCues: The more vertical your torso, the more shoulder-focused the move.
Elbows track at about 45°, not flared straight out.
commonMistakes: Letting hips drop into a regular push-up position
Head bending forward instead of crown going down

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
setupSteps: Stand tall, dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing your body.
Soft bend in the elbows.
executionSteps: Raise both arms out to the sides until they're roughly parallel to the floor.
Lead with the elbows, not the hands.
Pause briefly.
Lower with control.
coachingCues: Imagine pouring water from the dumbbells at the top.
No swinging — strict form, lighter weights.
Soft elbow bend stays the same throughout.
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weights up
Going too heavy and shrugging instead of raising

name: Dumbbell Row (One Arm)
setupSteps: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a bench.
Other foot on the floor for stability.
Holding a dumbbell in the free hand, let it hang straight down.
Back flat, parallel to the floor.
executionSteps: Pull the dumbbell up toward your hip — elbow leads, not the hand.
Squeeze your shoulder blade toward your spine at the top.
Lower the weight under control to a full stretch.
All reps on one side, then switch.
coachingCues: Imagine starting a lawn mower.
Pull with the back, not the arm.
Don't twist your torso to lift heavier — keep hips square.
commonMistakes: Twisting the torso to use momentum
Pulling with the bicep instead of the back
Letting the back round at the bottom of the rep

name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row (Two Arms)
setupSteps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand, hinge at the hips so torso is roughly 45° to the floor.
Knees soft, back flat.
Arms hang straight down, palms facing each other or the body.
executionSteps: Pull both dumbbells up toward your hips, elbows leading.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower with control.
coachingCues: Long flat back — don't round.
Pull to the hips, not the chest.
Hold the hinge — don't stand up while you row.
commonMistakes: Standing up to use momentum
Rounding the lower back
Pulling with the arms only
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel this in your lower back rather than your upper back.

name: Inverted Row
setupSteps: Set up under a sturdy bar (or sturdy table edge) at roughly waist height.
Lie underneath, grab the bar with hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward or you.
Body forms a straight line, heels on the floor.
executionSteps: Pull your chest up toward the bar.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower with control.
coachingCues: Lower the bar (or raise your feet) to make it harder.
Squeeze the glutes — body stays as one straight piece.
Pull elbows to your hips, not your shoulders.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging
Pulling chin to bar instead of chest

name: Lat Pulldown (Machine)
setupSteps: Sit at the lat pulldown machine, knees secured under the pad.
Grab the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms forward.
Sit tall, slight backward lean (~10°).
executionSteps: Pull the bar down toward your upper chest.
Squeeze your shoulder blades down and together.
Slowly let the bar return to full stretch overhead.
coachingCues: Pull elbows down and back, not the bar with your hands.
Stop the bar at upper chest, not lower.
Don't lean back to use momentum.
commonMistakes: Pulling the bar to the lap using lean and momentum
Flaring elbows wide
Going too heavy and losing position

name: Assisted Pull-Up (Band or Machine)
setupSteps: Loop a resistance band over a pull-up bar; pull the loose end down through itself.
Place one foot or knee in the band loop for assistance.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms forward.
(Or use the assisted pull-up machine with appropriate weight stack.)
executionSteps: Hang at full stretch.
Pull yourself up by driving the elbows down until your chin clears the bar.
Lower with control.
coachingCues: Pull elbows down to your sides, not back.
Engage your back before your arms.
Use a thicker band for more help, thinner for less.
commonMistakes: Kipping (using leg swing for momentum)
Stopping short of chin over bar

name: Kettlebell Swing (Russian)
setupSteps: Place a kettlebell on the floor about a foot in front of you.
Stand with feet just wider than shoulder-width.
Hinge at the hips, grab the kettlebell with both hands.
Tilt it slightly toward you so the handle is angled.
executionSteps: Hike the kettlebell back between your legs (like snapping a football).
Snap your hips forward forcefully — the kettlebell floats up to about chest height on its own.
Don't lift it with your arms.
Let gravity bring it back down, hinge again, repeat.
coachingCues: It's a hip snap, not a squat and not an arm raise.
Squeeze the glutes hard at the top.
Wrists stay neutral — no flopping.
commonMistakes: Squatting the swing instead of hinging
Lifting with the arms
Going too high (above shoulder) before mastering form
safetyNotes: Master the hip hinge before swinging.
Use a clear space — at least 2m in front of you.

name: Forearm Plank
setupSteps: Forearms on the floor, elbows under shoulders.
Legs extended straight back, toes on the floor.
Body forms a straight line from head to heels.
executionSteps: Brace your core hard — imagine bracing for a punch.
Squeeze your glutes.
Hold the position for the target time.
coachingCues: Hips in line with shoulders — no sagging, no piking.
Breathe normally — don't hold your breath.
Quality over duration — stop when form breaks.
commonMistakes: Hips sagging
Hips piking up
Holding breath

name: Dead Bug
setupSteps: Lie on your back, arms straight up over your shoulders.
Knees bent at 90° over your hips, shins parallel to the floor.
Lower back gently pressed into the floor.
executionSteps: Slowly lower the opposite arm and opposite leg toward the floor — arm overhead, leg straight.
Stop just before lower back lifts off the floor.
Return to the start.
Alternate sides each rep.
coachingCues: Lower back glued to the floor throughout.
Slow and controlled — this is harder than it looks.
Breathe out as you extend.
commonMistakes: Lower back arching off the floor
Going too fast and losing control

name: Farmer Carry
setupSteps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Stand tall — shoulders back, chest up, ribs down.
Have a clear path of at least 10–20m.
executionSteps: Walk in a straight line at a controlled pace.
Steps short and steady — don't rush.
Keep tall posture throughout.
Walk for the target distance or time.
coachingCues: Don't let the weights pull your shoulders forward.
Brace your core like you're walking with a tray of drinks.
Breathe steadily.
commonMistakes: Slouching forward under the weight
Letting the dumbbells swing
Going too heavy too soon

name: Wall Sit
setupSteps: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
Walk your feet out about 50cm in front of you, hip-width apart.
Slide down the wall until your thighs are parallel to the floor — knees at 90°.
executionSteps: Hold the position for the target time.
Breathe steadily.
coachingCues: Knees over ankles, not over toes.
Whole back stays in contact with the wall.
If 90° is too hard, sit a bit higher to start.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward
Hands resting on thighs (cheat)
Holding breath

name: Cat-Cow
setupSteps: Start on hands and knees — wrists under shoulders, knees under hips.
Spine in a neutral, flat-back position.
executionSteps: Inhale: drop your belly toward the floor, lift your chest and tailbone (Cow).
Exhale: round your back toward the ceiling, tuck your chin and tailbone (Cat).
Move slowly with your breath for the target reps or time.
coachingCues: Move from the spine, not just the head.
Breath leads the movement.
commonMistakes: Rushing the tempo
Only moving the neck

name: World's Greatest Stretch
setupSteps: Start in a standing position with space in front of you.
executionSteps: Step one foot forward into a deep lunge.
Place the same-side hand on the floor inside the front foot.
Reach the opposite hand up toward the ceiling, rotating the torso open.
Hold for 2 breaths.
Return and repeat on the other side.
coachingCues: Try to lengthen, not just rotate.
Eyes follow the top hand.
commonMistakes: Rushing the rotation
Back knee dropping all the way down (turn it into a lunge first, then rotate)
